docs.title: Attack Pack Structure
docs.text:
Each attack in attacks/<name>/ is a self-contained directory:

attacks/<name>/
├── plugin.yaml          # Plugin manifest (name, type, class, version)
├── evidence.yaml        # Historical basis, scope, and assumptions
├── <name>.py            # Attack logic (implements AttackPlugin)
└── (optional) *.sol     # Foundry scripts

docs.title: Scenario Structure
docs.text:
Each scenario in scenarios/<name>/:

scenarios/<name>/
└── scenario.yaml        # Environment definition (chains, steps, assertions)

docs.title: 3. Running Tests
docs.text:
# Run all unit tests (fast — no external deps needed)
pytest tests/unit/ -q

# Run with verbose output
pytest tests/unit/ -v

# Run a specific test file
pytest tests/unit/test_config.py -v

# Run with coverage
pytest tests/unit/ --cov=crucible

# Run docs link validation (checks all internal .md links resolve)
pytest tests/unit/test_docs_links.py -v

# Run dashboard e2e tests (if Streamlit installed)
pytest tests/unit/test_dashboard_e2e.py -v

# Run integration tests (requires Foundry)
pytest tests/integration/ -m requires_foundry

Current test count: 798 passing (all unit tests).

docs.title: Adding a New Attack (Quick Path)
docs.text:
crucible attack extract <tx_hash> --name my_attack --chain ethereum

This auto-generates the complete scaffold: - attacks/my_attack/plugin.yaml - attacks/my_attack/evidence.yaml - attacks/my_attack/my_attack.py - scenarios/my_attack_pre_exploit/scenario.yaml

docs.title: Adding a New Attack (Manual Path)
docs.text:
mkdir -p attacks/my_attack
# Create plugin.yaml, my_attack.py, evidence.yaml
mkdir -p scenarios/my_attack_pre_exploit
# Create scenario.yaml

crucible plugin validate attacks/my_attack

docs.title: Adding a New Chain
docs.text:
1. Add the chain to testbed.yaml under chains: (see ethereum for reference)
  2. Add RPC providers under rpc_providers: (at least one archive node)
  3. Create or update a topology file in topologies/ that references the new chain

docs.title: Adding a New Topology
docs.text:
Create topologies/<name>.yaml:

name: custom
chains: [ethereum, polygon]
bridge: null

docs.title: Adding a New Bridge Plugin
docs.text:
1. Create bridges/<name>/ with plugin.yaml + implementation
  2. Implement BridgePlugin interface from crucible.sdk
  3. Add bridge address map to abis/<name>/

docs.title: Authoring a New Attack Pack
docs.text:
All 6 benchmark packs are now complete at their respective fidelity levels. To author a new attack:

  1. Find exploit transaction hash on Etherscan/BscScan
  2. Set fork_block = exploit block - 1 in scenario
  3. Verify affected contract addresses are contracts (not EOAs) on Etherscan!
  4. Use crucible attack extract <tx_hash> --name <name> to auto-generate the scaffold
  5. Add concrete steps to scenario.yaml
  6. Run determinism check: run twice, crucible diff <run_a> <run_b>

docs.title: 5. CLI Reference (Quick)
docs.text:
Command Purpose crucible init Initialize workspace crucible doctor Validate environment (Python, Foundry, config, RPC) crucible status Show running chains crucible fork --topology <name> Start Anvil forks crucible setup --topology <name> Fork + bootstrap + capture baseline crucible experiment run <file.yaml> Formal research run crucible reset Soft reset (<5s) crucible diff <run_a> <run_b> Compare two runs crucible history List experiment history crucible export <run_id> Bundle a run for publication crucible clean Kill chains + remove temp state crucible dashboard Launch Streamlit UI

For full reference: docs/CLI_REFERENCE.md

docs.title: 6. Dashboard Overview
docs.text:
The Streamlit dashboard (dashboard/app.py) provides a browser UI for all CLI capabilities:

Panels (19 total):

Panel Category Panels Purpose Overview 🔬 Overview Project summary and quick actions Experiments [bolt] Run Experiment, 🎛️ Control Panel, 📊 Experiment History Run and manage experiments Monitoring 📡 Chain Monitor, 🌐 Topology View, 🧠 Agent Reasoning Observe chain/environment state Analysis 🔍 Run Details, 📐 Compare Runs, 📜 Log Browser, 💰 Estimates Analyze experiment results Diagnostics 🩺 Doctor, 📋 Evidence Validate environment and evidence Tools 💾 Checkpoints, [bolt] Attack Extract, 📦 Bundle Management, 🗃️ Cache Management, 📚 ABI Registry, 🔌 Plugin Validation Maintenance utilities

To launch: crucible dashboard or streamlit run dashboard/app.py

For full panel documentation: docs/DASHBOARD_GUIDE.md

docs.title: 7. Publication Workflow
docs.text:
# 1. Run with publication gates enabled
crucible experiment run experiments/attack.yaml --publication-mode

# 2. Export the bundle
crucible export <run_id> --format tar-gz

# 3. Verify the export
crucible verify datasets/<run_id>

# 4. Archive on Zenodo
# See docs/ZENODO_ARCHIVE_GUIDE.md for details

Each export bundle is self-contained with: - run_manifest.json (full provenance record) - run.jsonl, events.jsonl, storage_diffs.jsonl, gas_profile.jsonl - log_integrity.json (SHA-256 hashes) - REPRODUCE.md (exact commands to reproduce)

docs.title: 8. Stray Directories Explained
docs.text:
These directories are intentionally kept in the project root:

Directory Contents Purpose scripts/ 8 shell scripts Artifact evaluation, Docker validation, cross-provider reproduction notebooks/ experiment_analysis.ipynb Jupyter notebook for post-run analysis patches/ README.md (empty scaffold) Future patch validation packs plugins/ (empty) Future plugin development cache/ solidity-files-cache.json Foundry solc cache (gitignored) datasets/ (empty) Exported publication bundles (gitignored) states/ Anvil state files Runtime state (gitignored except baseline YAML) logs/ Experiment run logs Generated per run (gitignored) .fork_cache/ Anvil RPC cache Cached RPC responses (gitignored) lib/ forge-std/ Foundry standard library (git submodule) out/ Compiled contracts Foundry build output (gitignored)

docs.title: Technical
docs.text:
• Windows compatibility: Bash scripts in scripts/ require Git Bash or WSL. The crucible CLI works on Windows via uv run crucible.
  • Free-tier RPC limits: Archive-node RPC providers (LlamaRPC, Ankr, PublicNode) have rate limits of 10-25 req/s. Paid providers (Alchemy, Infura) are recommended for multi-chain benchmarks.
  • BSC/Polygon archive RPCs: Some multi-chain attack packs (Poly Network) require paid archive RPCs for BSC or Polygon. The Ethereum-only portions work on free tier.
  • Docker on Windows: Docker Compose networking may require additional configuration on Windows. See docs/internal/DOCKER_IMPLEMENTATION.md.

docs.title: Research Content
docs.text:
• 6/6 benchmark packs at target fidelity: All attacks are complete at their declared fidelity levels. Horizon and BNB Token Hub were upgraded from scaffolds to L2. Multichain correctly classified as L1 (MPC infrastructure compromise, beyond EVM scope).
  • 3 bridge plugins: Nomad (ethereum + polygon), Poly Network (ethereum + polygon + bsc), Wormhole (ethereum + polygon). Each deploys real Solidity contracts with vulnerability pre-configured.
  • Poly Network multi-chain: 3-chain topology available (Ethereum + Polygon + BSC); determinism proven across all 3 chains. BSC/Polygon archive nodes at exploit blocks require paid RPCs.
  • No Foundry integration tests: The tests/integration/ directory exists as a scaffold but requires a live Anvil instance to run.

docs.title: Code
docs.text:
• No type checking enforced: pyproject.toml has pyright config but it's not part of the regular test suite.
  • No integration tests running: Only unit tests are run in the main test suite. Integration and scenario tests require Foundry and/or RPC access.

docs.title: Priority 1: Author New Attack Packs
docs.text:
All 6 benchmark packs are complete. To author new attacks:

  1. Use crucible attack extract <tx_hash> to auto-generate a scaffold
  2. Verify contract addresses on Etherscan (confirmed contracts, not EOAs)
  3. Write the exploit plugin following patterns in existing packs
  4. Run determinism check — run twice, compare with crucible diff

docs.title: Priority 2: Live Anvil Fork Validation
docs.text:
Run the 3 completed benchmark packs (Wormhole, Nomad, Poly Network) against actual Anvil forks:

crucible setup --topology single
crucible experiment run experiments/wormhole_replay.yaml
crucible reset
crucible experiment run experiments/wormhole_replay.yaml
crucible diff DS-2026-XXXXX DS-2026-XXXXX

Verify: storage diffs match, events match, gas matches, outcome matches.

docs.title: Priority 3: Cross-Provider Reproduction
docs.text:
Run the full pipeline on a second machine/environment to claim the USENIX AE "Reproduced" badge:

# On machine 2:
git clone <repo>
uv sync
cp .env.example .env  # fill in RPC URLs
crucible doctor
crucible verify datasets/<run_id>

docs.title: Priority 4: Type Checking & Linting
docs.text: Run type checking and linting locally as part of development: - ruff check crucible/ tests/ — linting - pyright crucible/ — type checking (optional, may not pass cleanly)

docs.title: Priority 5: Documentation Improvements
docs.text:
• Add more case studies to docs/case_studies/ as packs are completed
  • Keep docs/internal/IMPLEMENTATION_STATUS.md updated as packs progress
  • Update README.md badge links when CI is configured

docs.title: What Was Cleaned Up
docs.text:
This section documents what was removed or moved during the June 2026 cleanup:

Removed (not for publication): - streamlit_app.py — deprecated entry point (replaced by dashboard/app.py) - crucible_assessment_and_critique.md — internal review - crucible_code_quality_review.md — internal review - final_architecture_review.md — internal review - final_review.md — internal review - somandteam_review2.md — internal review - CODEBASE_REVIEW.md — internal review - REMEDIATION_PLAN.md — internal remediation plan - docs/archive/ — 12 superseded/outdated docs

Moved to docs/design/: - crucible_implementation_spec.md (127K canonical design spec) - crucible_research_overview.md (research overview)

Fixed: - docker-compose.yml: streamlit_app.py → dashboard/app.py - README.md: test count 277 → 798, comprehensive doc table - .env.example: updated with clean template and comments - tests/unit/test_docs_links.py: handles subdirectories properly

docs.title: Testing
docs.text:
Run unit tests and smoke tests locally (no external services needed):

# Unit tests
docker compose run --rm orchestrator bash -c "uv run pytest tests/unit/ -v"

# Full smoke test (from repo root)
docker compose build
docker compose run --rm orchestrator init
docker compose run --rm --entrypoint bash orchestrator -c "
  rm -f states/*.meta.json && \
  uv run crucible fork --topology single && \
  uv run crucible experiment run experiments/demo_experiment.yaml --no-baseline
"
docker compose run --rm orchestrator logs list
docker compose run --rm orchestrator history --rebuild

Note: CI pipelines have been removed — this is a local-only project. All testing is done on-device.

docs.title: Managing Fork Blocks Per Experiment
docs.text:
Each attack pack requires a specific fork block on Ethereum (and possibly other chains). testbed.yaml has a single fork_block per chain, so you need testbed.local.yaml (gitignored) to switch between experiments:

# testbed.local.yaml — deep-merged with testbed.yaml
chains:
  ethereum:
    fork_block: 15259100  # Nomad Bridge fork
Attack Ethereum Polygon BSC Nomad Bridge 15,259,100 — — Wormhole Guardian 14,125,553 — — Poly Network 12,996,693 16,332,982 9,755,172 Horizon Multisig 14,399,491 — — BNB Token Hub — — 22,166,659 Multichain Router 16,329,241 — —

Workflow:

# 1. Edit testbed.local.yaml with the fork block for your target experiment
# 2. Fork the chain with the correct topology
crucible fork --topology <name>

# 3. If the fork block is wrong (contract has no code), Crucible's EOA check
#    will catch it with a clear error message via `crucible evidence check`

docs.title: Running Determinism Checks
docs.text:
Proving an experiment is deterministic is a two-run workflow:

# Run 1:
docker compose run --rm --entrypoint bash orchestrator -c "
  rm -f states/*.meta.json && \
  uv run crucible fork --topology single && \
  uv run crucible experiment run experiments/nomad_replay.yaml --no-baseline
"
# Note run ID A (e.g., DS-2026-00001)

# Run 2:
docker compose run --rm --entrypoint bash orchestrator -c "
  rm -f states/*.meta.json && \
  uv run crucible fork --topology single && \
  uv run crucible experiment run experiments/nomad_replay.yaml --no-baseline
"
# Note run ID B (e.g., DS-2026-00002)

# Compare:
docker compose run --rm orchestrator diff DS-2026-00001 DS-2026-00002

Expected output: all 4 metrics true (storage, events, gas, balances).

docs.title: Daily Start
docs.text:
crucible status --disk
crucible plugin list
crucible abi validate
crucible cache inspect

Check for:

  • orphaned chain metadata
  • large state/cache growth
  • invalid plugins
  • invalid ABI JSON
  • cache entries with stale provider/block identity

docs.title: Start A Research Session
docs.text:
crucible fork --topology <topology>
crucible baseline verify <scenario>

If no baseline exists:

crucible baseline capture <scenario> --topology <topology>

docs.title: L4 Research Session (With Bridge Plugin)
docs.text:
# 1. Validate the bridge plugin
crucible plugin list --type bridge
crucible plugin validate bridges/nomad_bridge

# 2. Fork with bridge topology (deploys contracts to all chains automatically)
crucible fork --topology nomad_bridge

# 3. Run experiment with fidelity enforcement
crucible experiment run experiments/my_l4_experiment.yaml

# 4. Check cross-chain artifacts
crucible logs list
cat logs/<experiment>/<run_id>/bridge/cross_chain.jsonl

Available bridge topologies: - nomad_bridge — ethereum + polygon (Nomad Replica) - poly_network_bridge — ethereum + polygon + bsc (EthCrossChainManager/Data) - wormhole_bridge — ethereum + polygon (WormholeCore/TokenBridge)

docs.title: Reset Between Runs
docs.text: crucible reset --scenario <scenario>

docs.title: Stop Everything
docs.text: crucible clean

docs.title: Cleanup Disk
docs.text:
crucible clean --checkpoints
crucible clean --cache
crucible clean --logs-older-than 30
crucible status --disk

docs.title: Rebuild History
docs.text:
crucible history --rebuild
crucible history --limit 20
crucible history --tag wormhole --outcome success  # filter by tag + outcome

docs.title: Auto-Extract Attack Packs
docs.text:
# From a known exploit transaction hash
crucible attack extract <tx_hash> --name my_attack --chain ethereum

# Validate the generated pack
crucible plugin validate attacks/my_attack

docs.title: Export & Verify Bundles
docs.text:
crucible export <run_id> --format tar-gz
crucible bundle hash datasets/<run_id>
crucible verify datasets/<run_id>

docs.title: Compare Run Results
docs.text: crucible diff DS-2026-00001 DS-2026-00002

docs.title: Safe Previews
docs.text:
crucible --dry-run clean --all
crucible --dry-run reset --scenario demo_scenario --mode hard

docs.title: Port In Use (Orphaned Processes)
docs.text:
ERROR: port 8545 is already in use for chain ethereum

Cause: A previous crucible fork or manual anvil instance is still running. Each uv run crucible fork spawns a process tree that can persist as orphans: 

uv run crucible fork --topology <name>
  └── crucible fork --topology <name>
       └── anvil --port 8545 ...
When the parent uv run is killed (by timeout, crash, or Ctrl+C), the children may survive.

Recovery steps:

# 1. Find what's on the port
fuser -v 8545/tcp
# or: ss -tlnp | grep 8545

# 2. Kill the entire process tree
#    Kill the anvil child first, then the crucible fork parent
kill -9 <anvil_PID>
kill -9 <crucible_fork_PID>

# 3. OR nuke everything at once (faster)
pkill -9 -f 'anvil' 2>/dev/null
pkill -9 -f 'crucible fork' 2>/dev/null
sleep 2

# 4. Remove stale meta files
rm -f states/*.meta.json

# 5. Verify all ports free
ss -tlnp | grep -E '854[5-8]' || echo 'All free'

If it keeps happening: Check for zombie processes, tmux sessions, or Docker containers managing the process.

Port override (multi-researcher environments): If another researcher legitimately owns the port, override it in testbed.local.yaml:

chains:
  ethereum:
    port: 8555  # use a different port

Prevention: Always run crucible clean --all between experiments, and use consistent port configuration.

docs.title: Zombie (Defunct) Processes
docs.text:
# ps aux shows: [anvil] <defunct> with state Z

Cause: A zombie process has exited but hasn't been reaped by its parent. Zombies can't be killed (SIGKILL has no effect) and don't hold ports.

Detection: 

ps aux | grep defunct | grep anvil

Handling: Crucible's chain_manager now detects zombies via /proc/<pid>/status (Linux) and treats them as already terminated. They will be reaped by init (PID 1) when their parent dies.

docs.title: Port Keeps Getting Reoccupied After Kill
docs.text:
Symptom: You free the port, verify it's free, but the next fork command says it's occupied again.

Likely causes: 1. Process tree — Killing anvil doesn't kill the parent crucible fork (which may restart it) 2. Previous fork still running — A uv run crucible fork from an earlier command is still alive 3. Not waiting long enough — The port may be in TIME_WAIT state (30-120s after kill)

Fix: Use the full cleanup procedure above (pkill + remove meta + verify). For TIME_WAIT, wait 30s or use a different port.

docs.title: Baseline Mismatch
docs.text:
Do not overwrite the baseline immediately. First identify why the state changed:

crucible baseline verify <scenario>

If the baseline should change, capture a new one and document the reason.

docs.title: RPC Too Slow Or Expensive
docs.text: Use cached fork mode (fork_cache: { cache_policy: strict }) or local archive mode. Run crucible cache inspect to check cache state.

docs.title: Invalid Plugin
docs.text:
crucible plugin validate attacks/<name>

Fix manifest, evidence, entry file, or class inheritance issues.

docs.title: Docker: "sh -c" Command Fails
docs.text:
$ docker compose run --rm orchestrator sh -c "..."
Fails with: crucible: error: argument command: invalid choice: 'sh'

Cause: The orchestrator's Docker entrypoint is ["uv", "run", "crucible"], not a shell. Running sh -c passes sh as a subcommand to crucible.

Fix: Override the entrypoint with --entrypoint bash:

docker compose run --rm --entrypoint bash orchestrator -c "
  uv run crucible fork --topology single && \
  uv run crucible experiment run experiments/nomad_replay.yaml --no-baseline
"

docs.title: Docker: Stale Meta File Errors
docs.text:
ERROR: RPC call failed for anvil_dumpState at http://127.0.0.1:8547: Connection refused

Cause: The states/ Docker volume persists .meta.json files from previous forks. When you switch topologies (e.g., from poly_network back to single), stale meta files reference chains that aren't running.

Fix: Always clean stale meta files before forking:

docker compose run --rm --entrypoint bash orchestrator -c "
  rm -f states/*.meta.json && \
  uv run crucible fork --topology single
"

docs.title: Docker: Chain Not Ready (Connection Refused)
docs.text:
ERROR: chain bsc did not become ready within 30s on http://127.0.0.1:8547: Connection refused

Cause 1: The RPC URL in testbed.yaml is an environment variable (${BSC_RPC_URL}) that isn't set.

Fix: Check the env var: 

docker compose run --rm --entrypoint bash orchestrator -c 'echo "BSC_RPC_URL=$BSC_RPC_URL"'

If empty, set it in .env.

Cause 2: The RPC provider doesn't support archive queries at the configured fork block.

Fix: Test archive depth: 

docker compose run --rm --entrypoint bash orchestrator -c '\
  curl -s -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d "{\"jsonrpc\":\"2.0\",\"id\":1,\"method\":\"eth_getBlockByNumber\",\"params\":[\"0x94E334\",false]}" \
  https://your-rpc-provider\
'

See docs/RPC_PROVIDER_GUIDE.md for archive-compatible providers.

docs.title: Fork Block Wrong For Experiment
docs.text:
Attack verification failed: EthCrossChainManager has no code at fork block 12996062

Cause: The testbed.yaml fork_block is set for a different experiment (e.g., Wormhole instead of Poly Network).

Fix: Check which fork block each experiment needs and update testbed.yaml or create a testbed.local.yaml override:

# testbed.local.yaml
chains:
  ethereum:
    fork_block: 12996693  # Poly Network fork
Attack Ethereum Fork Block Nomad Bridge 15259100 Wormhole Guardian 14125553 Poly Network 12996693

docs.title: Contract Address Verification
docs.text:
Attack verification failed: ContractName has no code at fork block

Cause: The contract address in the attack pack might be wrong — several attack packs had addresses that were actually EOAs, not contracts.

Fix: Always verify addresses on Etherscan before using them:

  1. Go to Etherscan
  2. Paste the address
  3. Verify it shows "Contract" (not "Address (EOA)")
  4. Check the contract has non-zero transaction history

docs.title: Foundry Not Available
docs.text:
crucible: error: anvil not found on PATH

Inside Docker: Foundry is pre-installed. If missing, rebuild: 

docker compose build

Locally: Install Foundry: 

curl -L https://foundry.paradigm.xyz | bash
foundryup

docs.title: Phase 5 — Post-Publication Polish
docs.text: Context: The Phase 2–4 strategic plan (17 items) is complete and Crucible has been reviewed as "ready to publish." This document captures the 4 suggestions from the architectural review (thearchitectural_and_code_review.md) as a Phase 5 roadmap.

docs.title: Current Status
docs.text: Suggestion Status Effort Priority 1. Pre-flight live state assertions [ok] Done (validate_evidence_against_chain()) Small P3 — complete 2. crucible verify <bundle.tar.gz> [ok] Done (auto-extract + verify) Small P3 — complete 3. Checked-in golden state fixtures for CI ⏭️ Deferred — local-only project, no CI Medium P1 — skipped 4. EOA / contract checks during extract [ok] Done (eth_getCode in init + extract) Trivial P2 — complete

docs.title: P2: EOA Checks During Attack Extract and Init
docs.text:
Files changed: crucible/commands/attack.py, crucible/extract.py

  • crucible attack init --from-tx now calls eth_getCode on the target address — prints a green confirmation for contracts, a yellow WARNING for EOAs
  • crucible attack extract now fast-fails with ExtractError if the to address has no code (likely an EOA)
  • 4 new unit tests in tests/unit/test_attack_from_tx.py

docs.title: P3: crucible verify <bundle.tar.gz> Auto-Extraction
docs.text:
Files changed: crucible/commands/verify.py

  • Auto-detects .tar.gz, .tgz, .zip, .tar archives
  • Extracts to temp dir, verifies, cleans up (wrapped in try/finally)
  • 5 new unit tests in tests/unit/test_verify.py
  • CLI_REFERENCE.md updated with archive examples

docs.title: P3: Standalone crucible evidence check Command
docs.text:
Files changed: crucible/evidence.py, crucible/commands/evidence.py, crucible/commands/__init__.py, crucible/experiment.py

  • validate_evidence_against_chain() extracted from ExperimentRunner into crucible/evidence.py as a standalone function
  • crucible evidence check <attack> CLI command with --fork mode (forks, validates, cleans up in one step)
  • ExperimentRunner._validate_evidence_against_chain() now delegates to the standalone function
  • 10 new unit tests in tests/unit/test_evidence.py
  • 2 new CLI smoke tests in tests/unit/test_cli_smoke.py

docs.title: Deferred: P1 Golden State Fixtures
docs.text:
Decision (June 8, 2026): Deferred — the project runs locally without CI, so hermetic state fixtures provide no local benefit. All related files have been cleaned up. The infrastructure (`requires_golden_fixture` marker, test scaffold, fixture directory) was removed along with the fixture files.

If CI is ever added, golden fixtures can be re-implemented by: 1. Running anvil_dumpState at a pinned fork block 2. Compressing the state JSON with gzip 3. Checking the fixture into tests/fixtures/ 4. Writing a hermetic end-to-end test using @pytest.mark.requires_golden_fixture

docs.title: Plugin Development Guide
docs.text: Audience: Researchers and developers writing attack, bridge, or agent plugins for Crucible.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Crucible supports three plugin types:

Type Base Class Purpose Attack AttackPlugin Reproduce a historical exploit Bridge BridgePlugin Simulate a cross-chain bridge protocol Agent AgentPlugin Create an LLM-driven autonomous agent

All plugins are Python classes that subclass the appropriate base class from crucible.sdk. They are discovered automatically by the PluginRegistry by scanning attacks/, bridges/, and plugins/ directories.

docs.title: Plugin Structure
docs.text:
Every plugin follows this directory convention:

attacks/my_exploit/
├── plugin.yaml          # Plugin manifest (required)
├── evidence.yaml        # Evidence declaration (required for attacks)
├── my_exploit.py        # Plugin implementation
└── ...                  # Additional files (ABI, bytecode, etc.)

docs.title: Plugin Manifest (plugin.yaml)
docs.text:
Crucible supports a modern manifest format that clearly declares dependencies. (The legacy format with class_name and chains_required is deprecated but still supported).

name: my_exploit
version: 1.0.0
type: attack           # attack | bridge | agent
entry: my_exploit.py
class: MyExploit       # The Python class name

# Optional metadata
author: "Security Researcher"
reproduction: {}       # Reserved for reproduction-specific metadata

# Dependency Requirements
requires:
  crucible: ">=0.1.0"
  chains: [ethereum]   # Required chains to run this plugin
  topology: single     # Required topology (e.g., single, bridge)

docs.title: Evidence Declaration (evidence.yaml)
docs.text:
Every attack plugin must include an evidence.yaml:

incident:
  name: "My Exploit"
  date: "2024-01-01"
  affected_bridge: "MyBridge"
  funds_lost_usd: 1000000
  summary: "Description of the vulnerability"

sources:
  advisories:
    - "https://example.com/advisory"
  postmortems:
    - "https://example.com/postmortem"

historical_scope:
  chains: ["ethereum"]
  fork_blocks:
    ethereum: 15000000
  exploit_transactions:
    - "0xabc..."
  affected_contracts:
    VulnerableContract: "0x123..."
  attacker_addresses:
    - "0xabc..."

reproduction_scope:
  fidelity_level: L2
  exact_tx_replay: false
  modeled_components:
    - "Fork-state call to vulnerable contract"
  excluded_components:
    - "Non-EVM chain state"

acceptance:
  required_assertions:
    - name: vulnerability_exists
      description: "The vulnerable state exists at fork block"
      expected: true
    - name: exploit_succeeds
      description: "The exploit transaction does not revert"
      expected: true

docs.title: Step 1: Scaffold
docs.text:
# Create from a real exploit transaction (populates evidence.yaml from on-chain data)
crucible attack extract 0xabc... --name my_exploit

# Or create from template (empty scaffold)
crucible attack init my_exploit --chain ethereum --fidelity L2 --author "Your Name"

docs.title: Step 2: Implement the Plugin
docs.text:
from crucible.sdk import AttackPlugin, AttackResult, ExperimentContext, VerificationResult


class MyExploit(AttackPlugin):
    def metadata(self) -> dict:
        return {
            "name": "my_exploit",
            "version": "1.0.0",
            "type": "attack",
            "fidelity_level": "L2",
            "damage_usd": 1000000,
        }

    def execute(self, context: ExperimentContext, phase: int | None = None) -> AttackResult:
        # 1. Get a client for the forked chain
        client = context.chains.runtime_for("ethereum")

        # 2. Check contract exists
        code = client.get_code("0x123...")
        if not code or code == "0x":
            return AttackResult(success=False, phase_results=[
                {"step": "check_contract", "passed": False, "detail": "no code"}
            ])

        # 3. Forge exploit calldata
        calldata = "0x..."

        # 4. Send exploit transaction
        tx_hash = client.call("eth_sendTransaction", [{
            "to": "0x123...",
            "data": calldata,
        }])

        # 5. Check result
        receipt = client.call("eth_getTransactionReceipt", [tx_hash])
        success = receipt and receipt.get("status") == "0x1"

        return AttackResult(success=success, phase_results=[
            {"step": "check_contract", "passed": True},
            {"step": "send_exploit", "passed": success},
        ])

    def verify(self, context: ExperimentContext) -> VerificationResult:
        client = context.chains.runtime_for("ethereum")

        # Check on-chain state matches expectations
        balance = client.get_balance("0xattacker...")
        assertions = []
        errors = []

        if balance > 0:
            assertions.append({"name": "attacker_balance", "passed": True})
        else:
            errors.append("attacker balance did not increase")

        return VerificationResult(
            passed=len(errors) == 0,
            assertions=assertions,
            errors=errors,
        )

docs.title: Step 3: Create the Experiment
docs.text:
# experiments/my_exploit.yaml
name: my_exploit
scenario: my_scenario
attack: my_exploit
topology: single

docs.title: Step 4: Create the Scenario
docs.text:
# scenarios/my_scenario/scenario.yaml
phases:
  - name: setup
    steps:
      - type: log
        message: "Setting up exploit environment"
      - type: impersonate
        chain: ethereum
        account: "0xattacker..."
      - type: assert_balance
        chain: ethereum
        account: "0xattacker..."
        min_value: "1000000000000000000"  # 1 ETH

docs.title: Step 5: Run
docs.text:
crucible fork --topology single
crucible experiment run experiments/my_exploit.yaml

docs.title: The ExperimentContext
docs.text:
Every plugin method receives an ExperimentContext with these fields:

@dataclass
class ExperimentContext:
    config: TestbedConfig          # Full testbed configuration
    topology: TopologyConfig       # Active topology definition
    chains: ChainManager           # Chain lifecycle management
    log: LogEmitter | LogManager   # Artifact logging
    workspace: Path                # Project root path
    run_id: str                    # Unique run identifier (e.g., DS-2026-00042)
    run_config: dict               # Experiment-specific run config
    bridge_plugin: Any             # Deployed bridge plugin (if any)

docs.title: Accessing Chain State
docs.text:
# Get a JSON-RPC client for a chain
runtime = context.chains.runtime_for("ethereum")
client = JsonRpcClient(runtime.rpc_url)

# Read chain state
balance = client.get_balance("0x...")
code = client.get_code("0x...")
storage = client.get_storage_at("0x...", "0x0")
block = client.call("eth_getBlockByNumber", ["0x...", False])

# Send transactions
tx_hash = client.send_transaction({
    "to": "0x...",
    "data": "0x...",
    "from": "0x...",
})
receipt = client.call("eth_getTransactionReceipt", [tx_hash])

# Impersonate accounts (requires --auto-impersonate or manual anvil_impersonate)
client.impersonate_account("0x...")

docs.title: Logging
docs.text:
# Step logging (appears in run.jsonl)
context.log.step("message", phase="attack", action="exploit_execution", status="success")

# Event logging (appears in events.jsonl)
context.log.event(
    chain="ethereum",
    event_name="Transfer",
    address="0x...",
    topics=["0x..."],
    data="0x...",
)

# Storage diff logging
context.log.storage_diff(
    chain="ethereum",
    contract="0x...",
    slot="0x...",
    before="0x...",
    after="0x...",
)

# Gas logging
context.log.gas(step="exploit_tx", gas_used=21000, chain="ethereum")

docs.title: Writing a Bridge Plugin
docs.text:
Bridge plugins simulate cross-chain bridge protocols. They follow a full lifecycle:

from crucible.sdk import BridgePlugin, ExperimentContext


class MyBridge(BridgePlugin):
    def metadata(self) -> dict:
        return {
            "name": "my_bridge",
            "version": "1.0.0",
            "type": "bridge",
        }

    def deploy(self, context: ExperimentContext) -> dict[str, str]:
        # Deploy bridge contracts on all chains
        # Returns dict of chain_name -> deployed_address
        eth_client = context.chains.runtime_for("ethereum")
        # ... deploy contract ...
        return {"ethereum": "0xdeployed..."}

    def configure(self, context, config=None):
        # Set validator set, relayer addresses, guardian keys
        pass

    def connect(self, context):
        # Start message-passing simulation
        pass

    def send_message(self, context, source_chain, dest_chain, data, correlation_id=None):
        # Send a cross-chain message
        client = context.chains.runtime_for(source_chain)
        tx_hash = client.send_transaction({
            "to": context.bridge_address,
            "data": data,
        })
        return {"source_tx_hash": tx_hash, "correlation_id": correlation_id}

    def reset(self, context):
        # Reset bridge state to post-deploy baseline
        pass

    def destroy(self, context):
        # Tear down relayer and deployed contracts
        pass

docs.title: Bridge Plugin Manifest
docs.text:
# bridges/my_bridge/plugin.yaml
name: my_bridge
version: 1.0.0
type: bridge
entry: my_bridge.py
class: MyBridge

Bridge plugins also require evidence.yaml:

# bridges/my_bridge/evidence.yaml
protocol:
  name: "My Bridge Protocol"
  version: "1.0"

features:
  - "Cross-chain message passing"
  - "Validator set management"

limitations:
  - "No real relayer — simulation only"
  - "Single message per transaction"

chains: ["ethereum", "polygon"]

docs.title: Writing an Agent Plugin
docs.text:
Agent plugins replace traditional attack plugins with LLM-driven autonomous agents:

from crucible.sdk import AgentPlugin, ExperimentContext, VerificationResult
from crucible.agent_tools import AgentAction, AgentContext


class MyAgent(AgentPlugin):
    def metadata(self) -> dict:
        return {
            "name": "my_agent",
            "version": "1.0.0",
            "type": "agent",
        }

    def think(self, observation, context, agent_ctx):
        # Given the current observation, decide what action to take
        # Return an AgentAction, or None to end the loop
        return AgentAction(
            tool="get_balance",
            params={"address": "0x..."},
            reasoning="Check attacker balance",
        )

    def verify(self, context, agent_ctx):
        return VerificationResult(passed=True)

docs.title: Agent Experiment Config
docs.text:
# experiments/my_agent.yaml
name: my_agent
scenario: my_scenario
attack: my_agent
topology: single
run:
  agent_provider: openai        # openai | anthropic | ollama | mock
  agent_privilege: medium       # low | medium | high
  agent_allowed_accounts:
    - "0xattacker..."

docs.title: Attack Plugin
docs.text:
prepare() → execute() → verify() → rollback() → cleanup()
                      ↘ run() ↗          (convenience wrapper)
Method Called Purpose prepare() Before execute Set up attacker accounts, approve tokens, deploy helpers execute() Main execution Run the attack, return AttackResult verify() After execute Check on-chain state against expected results rollback() On failure Undo persistent changes not handled by snapshot restore cleanup() Always Remove temp files, kill helper processes

docs.title: Bridge Plugin
docs.text:
deploy() → configure() → connect() → send_message()* → reset() → destroy()
Method Called Purpose deploy() Once Deploy bridge contracts to all chains configure() After deploy Set validator set, relayer addresses, guardian keys connect() Before messaging Start message-passing simulation / relayer process send_message() Per message Send a cross-chain message reset() Between runs Reset bridge state to post-deploy baseline destroy() Teardown Tear down relayer and clean up deployed contracts

docs.title: Agent Plugin
docs.text:
prepare() → [observe() → think() → respond()]* → verify()
Method Called Purpose prepare() Once Set up agent environment observe() Per iteration Gather observations from the environment think() Per iteration Decide what action to take (return AgentAction or None) respond() Per iteration Process the result of the action verify() End Verify the agent achieved its objective

docs.title: 1. Always Return Structured Results
docs.text:
Always return AttackResult with phase_results containing individual step results:

return AttackResult(success=True, phase_results=[
    {"step": "check_contract", "passed": True, "detail": "Contract has code"},
    {"step": "exploit_execution", "passed": True, "detail": tx_hash},
])

Each phase result appears in the run manifest and is visible in crucible history.

docs.title: 2. Use the LogEmitter
docs.text:
Don't print() — use context.log.step():

context.log.step(
    "Verifying contract code at fork block",
    phase="attack", action="check_code", status="success",
    contract=CONTRACT_ADDRESS,
    fork_block=fork_block,
)

docs.title: 3. Handle Chain Unavailability Gracefully
docs.text:
When creating multi-chain plugins, wrap per-chain operations in try/except:

for chain_name in ["ethereum", "polygon"]:
    try:
        client = context.chains.runtime_for(chain_name)
        code = client.get_code(CONTRACT_ADDRESS)
        if code and code != "0x":
            results[chain_name] = True
    except Exception as exc:
        context.log.step(
            f"Skipping {chain_name}: {exc}",
            phase="attack", action="check_code", status="warning",
        )

docs.title: 4. Store Important Addresses as Constants
docs.text:
VULNERABLE_CONTRACT = "0x..."
ATTACKER_ADDRESS = "0x..."
FUNCTION_SELECTOR = "0x..."

docs.title: 5. Document Fidelity Constraints
docs.text:
In evidence.yaml, be honest about what your plugin cannot reproduce:

reproduction_scope:
  excluded_components:
    - "Solana-side validators (non-EVM — cannot fork)"
    - "Off-chain MPC key generation"

docs.title: 1. Double 0x Prefix in Bytecode
docs.text:
When deploying contracts, Foundry .json artifacts already include the 0x prefix:

# WRONG — double 0x prefix
bytecode = "0x" + artifact["bytecode"]  # artifact["bytecode"] already starts with 0x

# RIGHT
bytecode = artifact["bytecode"]

docs.title: 2. EOA vs Contract Addresses
docs.text:
Always verify that target addresses are contracts, not EOAs:

code = client.get_code(address)
if not code or code == "0x":
    # This is an EOA, not a contract!
    raise ValueError(f"Expected contract at {address}, got EOA")

docs.title: 3. Archive RPC Limitations
docs.text: Polygon and BSC archive RPCs at old fork blocks may not serve state for newly created addresses. This affects anvil_setBalance when bridge plugins try to fund deployer addresses. Always wrap per-chain deployment in try/except.

docs.title: 4. Non-EVM Boundaries
docs.text: Crucible is EVM-only. Attacks involving Solana, Cosmos, Harmony ONE, or Substrate chains must be documented in excluded_components — they cannot be reproduced on-chain.

docs.title: 1. Validate the Plugin Loads
docs.text:
crucible plugin validate attacks/my_exploit

This checks: - Plugin manifest (plugin.yaml) is well-formed - Entry point file exists - Class can be imported and instantiated - Evidence (evidence.yaml) has all required sections

docs.title: 2. Check Evidence Against Live Chain
docs.text:
crucible evidence check my_exploit --fork

This forks the chain at the configured block and verifies: - Fork block exists via eth_getBlockByNumber - Declared contracts have non-empty code via eth_getCode

docs.title: 3. Run the Experiment
docs.text:
crucible fork --topology single
crucible experiment run experiments/my_exploit.yaml

docs.title: 4. Verify Determinism
docs.text:
# Run twice
crucible experiment run experiments/my_exploit.yaml --name run-a
crucible experiment run experiments/my_exploit.yaml --name run-b

# Compare
crucible diff <run-a-id> <run-b-id>

All four metrics must be true: - storage_equal - events_equal - gas_equal - balances_equal

docs.title: 5. Export for Publication
docs.text:
# Export bundle
crucible export <run-id>

# Verify bundle integrity
crucible verify datasets/<run-id>

docs.title: Release Checklist
docs.text: Purpose: Step-by-step checklist for shipping a Crucible release. Last updated: June 16, 2026 Status: 🟢 Ready to ship — all verifiable items checked, runtime validation only remaining

docs.title: Pre-Release Validation
docs.text: Before tagging a release, run through this checklist in order.

docs.title: 1. Test Suite
docs.text:
# Full unit test suite — must pass 100%
python -m pytest tests/unit/ -q --tb=line
# Expected: 782 passed, 0 failed

# Docs link validation — all internal .md links must resolve
python -m pytest tests/unit/test_docs_links.py -q
# Expected: 32 passed, 0 failed

# CLI smoke tests — help output and command validation
python -m pytest tests/unit/test_cli_smoke.py -q
# Expected: 43 passed, 0 failed
  • All 782 unit tests pass — ⏳ needs environment with .venv access
  • All 32 docs link tests pass — ⏳ needs environment with .venv access
  • All 43 CLI smoke tests pass — ⏳ needs environment with .venv access

Note: The test count was updated from 808 → 782 to match actual def test_ function count (see W7 fix).

docs.title: 2. Plugin Validation
docs.text:
# All plugins must discover and load without errors
python -c "
from crucible.plugin import PluginRegistry
reg = PluginRegistry()
infos = reg.discover()
for info in infos:
    assert info.valid, f'{info.name}: {info.error}'
    if info.type == 'attack':
        plugin = reg.load(info.type, info.name)
print(f'All {len(infos)} plugins valid')
"
  • All 11 attack plugins load without errors — ⏳ needs environment with .venv access
  • All 4 bridge plugins load without errors — ⏳ needs environment with .venv access
  • Agent plugin loads without errors — ⏳ needs environment with .venv access

docs.title: 3. Docker Build
docs.text:
# Full build from clean state
docker compose build orchestrator
  • Docker image builds without errors — ⏳ needs Docker daemon
  • No README.md not found or similar context errors — ⏳ needs Docker daemon

docs.title: 4. Docker Smoke Test
docs.text:
Two options:

Option A — Blank fork (offline, no RPC needed):

# Clean slate + blank fork config
docker compose run --rm --entrypoint bash orchestrator -c "
  rm -f states/*.meta.json && \
  rm -rf .fork_cache/ethereum && \
  echo 'chains:\n  ethereum:\n    fork_block: null' > testbed.local.yaml && \
  uv run crucible fork --topology single && \
  uv run crucible experiment run experiments/demo_experiment.yaml --no-baseline && \
  rm -f testbed.local.yaml
"

Option B — Archive fork (requires RPC configured in .env):

docker compose run --rm --entrypoint bash orchestrator -c "
  rm -f states/*.meta.json && \
  uv run crucible fork --topology single && \
  uv run crucible experiment run experiments/demo_experiment.yaml --no-baseline
"

For offline CI environments without RPC, use Option A (blank fork). The topology: single uses ethereum with fork_block: 12996693 by default, which requires an archive RPC. The blank fork override sets fork_block: null to start Anvil as a standalone local EVM with zero external dependencies.

  • crucible doctor passes — ⏳ needs Docker or local Foundry
  • Demo experiment runs to completion — ⏳ needs Docker or local Foundry
  • Artifacts created at logs/demo_experiment/DS-2026-xxxxx/ — ⏳ needs Docker or local Foundry
  • report.md generated with non-empty content — ⏳ needs Docker or local Foundry
  • run_manifest.json contains valid provenance — ⏳ needs Docker or local Foundry

docs.title: 5. Attack Pack Validation (If RPC Available)
docs.text:
# Run one historical attack (requires archive RPC)
docker compose run --rm --entrypoint bash orchestrator -c "
  rm -f states/*.meta.json && \
  uv run crucible fork --topology single && \
  uv run crucible experiment run experiments/nomad_replay.yaml --no-baseline
"
  • Attack pack loads and executes — ⏳ needs Docker + archive RPC
  • Assertions pass (committedRoot_is_zero, process_call_succeeds, etc.) — ⏳ needs Docker + archive RPC
  • Artifact streams contain expected data — ⏳ needs Docker + archive RPC

docs.title: 6. Documentation Review
docs.text:
# Quick content checks
python -c "
readme = open('README.md').read()
assert '782 unit tests' in readme, 'README.md test count stale'
assert 'EVM bridge security research testbed' in readme
print('README.md content checks passed')
"
  • README.md test count matches actual (782) — ✅ Fixed in W7
  • No broken documentation links in README — ✅ All 27 broken links fixed in W2
  • Internal docs not exposed on public site — ✅ Removed from mkdocs.yml nav in W1
  • Landing page doesn't link to internal docs — ✅ Fixed in W3
  • CLI_REFERENCE.md matches actual commands — ✅ Verified in docs audit
  • FEATURE_GUIDE.md internal links correct — ✅ Fixed TECHNICAL_OVERVIEW.md and DESIGN_DECISIONS.md links
  • Fidelity tiering consistent across README, index.md, and evidence.yaml — ✅ Fixed in W5
  • No stale/incorrect content in tutorial — ✅ Fixed duplicated header in W15
  • CHANGELOG.md and CONTRIBUTING.md present — ✅ Created in W17
  • ARCHITECTURE.md module counts match actual implementation — ❌ 39 listed, actual 41 files in crucible/ — needs update at lines 176 and 1163
  • docs link tests — ⏳ needs .venv access to run

docs.title: 7. Configuration Review
docs.text:
• testbed.yaml has correct default fork blocks — ✅ Verified
  • pyproject.toml version is 0.4.0 — ✅ Current release target
  • .env.example has clean template (no real keys) — ✅ Verified (all placeholder values)
  • CITATION.cff version matches pyproject.toml (0.4.0) — ✅ Both now at 0.4.0

docs.title: 8. Git Status
docs.text:
git status                # Should be clean (no dirty files)
git log --oneline -5      # Recent commit history
  • Working tree is clean (no uncommitted changes) — ✅ All changes committed in 38cd399
  • All changes committed with descriptive messages — ✅ Commit 38cd399 with full W1-W17 changelog
  • Changes pushed to remote — ✅ Pushed to origin/main
  • Feature branch merged to main — ✅ Already on main

docs.title: 9. Version Bump
docs.text:
Update version in: - pyproject.toml — [project] → version - docs/ARCHITECTURE.md — Version field at top

# Check current version
grep 'version = ' pyproject.toml
grep 'Version:' docs/ARCHITECTURE.md | head -1

Semantic versioning: - Patch (0.4.0 → 0.4.1): Bug fixes, doc updates, no new features - Minor (0.4.0 → 0.5.0): New feature, backward compatible - Major (0.4.0 → 1.0.0): Breaking changes, public release

  • Version bumped in pyproject.toml — 📝 Skip if still 0.4.0
  • Version bumped in docs/ARCHITECTURE.md — 📝 Skip if still 0.4.0

docs.title: 10. Commit Version Bump
docs.text:
git add .
git commit -m "Bump version to X.Y.Z and finalize documentation"
git push
  • All changes committed — ✅ Commit 38cd399
  • Changes pushed to remote — ✅ Pushed to origin/main

docs.title: 11. Tag Release
docs.text:
git tag -a v0.4.0 -m "Crucible v0.4.0 - Documentation completion, W1-W17 fixes, release readiness"
git push origin v0.4.0
  • Tag created and pushed — ✅ v0.4.0 exists locally
  • Tag pushed to remote — ✅ v0.4.0 pushed and up-to-date on origin

docs.title: 12. Zenodo Archive (if applicable)
docs.text:
If the repo is linked to Zenodo: 1. Visit zenodo.org/deposit 2. Wait for the auto-created draft (5-10 min after tag push) 3. Review metadata (title, authors, description) 4. Set access level (Restricted for AE, Open for publication) 5. Publish

  • Zenodo draft created and reviewed — 📝 After tag push
  • DOI assigned — 📝 After Zenodo publish
  • CITATION.cff updated with DOI — 📝 Post-release cleanup
  • CITATION.cff change committed (post-tag) — 📝 Post-release cleanup

docs.title: 13. Push Tag to GitHub
docs.text:
git push origin v0.4.0
  • Tag pushed to remote — 📝 After all checks pass
  • Zenodo auto-creates draft record (if linked) — 📝 After tag push

docs.title: 14. Verify Zenodo DOI Works
docs.text:
# Test the DOI resolves
curl -sI https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.XXXXXXX | head -5
  • DOI resolves to the Zenodo record — 📝 Post-release
  • Zenodo record has correct metadata — 📝 Post-release

docs.title: 15. Update Documentation
docs.text:
• Update docs/AE_WALKTHROUGH.md with new release tag — 📝 Post-release
  • Update README.md if any badges or links changed — 📝 Post-release
  • Update docs/ZENODO_ARCHIVE_GUIDE.md if workflow changed — 📝 Post-release

docs.title: Quick Reference: Common Commands
docs.text:
# Before release — full validation in one command
python -m pytest tests/unit/ -q --tb=line && \
python -m pytest tests/unit/test_docs_links.py -q --tb=line && \
python -m pytest tests/unit/test_cli_smoke.py -q --tb=line && \
echo "All checks passed"

# Docker smoke test
docker compose build orchestrator && \
docker compose run --rm --entrypoint bash orchestrator -c "
  rm -f states/*.meta.json && \
  uv run crucible fork --topology single && \
  uv run crucible experiment run experiments/demo_experiment.yaml --no-baseline
"

# Git tag and push
git tag -a v0.4.0 -m "message"
git push origin v0.4.0

docs.title: Crucible Testbed — Phase 2-4 Completion Report
docs.text: Date: June 8, 2026 Author: Codebuff AI Agent Audience: Expert Review Team

docs.title: Executive Summary
docs.text: This report documents the implementation of all 17 remaining items from the Crucible Testbed strategic plan (Phases 2-4). The implementation spans 12 source files modified, 10 test files created, and 12 documentation files consolidated.

docs.title: Key Metrics
docs.text: Metric Before After Delta Unit tests 559 669 +110 Docs link tests 22 22 [ok] All pass User-facing docs 31 27 Consolidation + new additions Internal docs 0 12 Consolidated Source files changed — 14 Items 5-17 + docs New test assertions — 110 Coverage added

docs.title: Integration Test Result
docs.text:
docker compose run --rm orchestrator experiment run experiments/demo_experiment.yaml --no-baseline --tier saved
→ [ok] PASS (exitCode: 0)
→ Artifacts at: logs/demo_experiment/DS-2026-00218/

docker compose run --rm orchestrator verify logs/demo_experiment/DS-2026-00218 --verbose --schemas
→ [ok] Verification: PASS (2 checks)
→ [ok] Schema validation: PASS (12 stream(s), 14 entries)

docs.title: Item 5: Name Flag for Runs
docs.text:
Effort: XS

Added --name flag to crucible experiment run for human-readable run aliases.

  • File: crucible/commands/experiment.py
  • Usage: crucible experiment run experiments/nomad_replay.yaml --name "nomad-v2-repro"
  • Storage: run_manifest.json → run_alias field, SQLite DB → run_alias column
  • Status: [ok] Complete

docs.title: Item 6: crucible verify Command
docs.text:
Effort: M

New crucible verify <path> command that auto-detects run vs bundle directories and re-computes SHA-256 hashes against integrity manifests.

  • Files: crucible/export.py (added verify_run(), verify()), crucible/commands/verify.py (new)
  • Detection: log_integrity.json = run directory, artifact_bundle_manifest.json = bundle, both = dual-pass
  • Reports: Hash mismatches, missing files, extra files (informational only)
  • Exit code: Non-zero on verification failure
  • Status: [ok] Complete

Updated docs/TUTORIAL.md to reflect all recent feature additions.

  • Status: [ok] Complete

Moved 12 internal/planning documents from docs/ to docs/internal/ to keep user-facing documentation clean.

  • Moved files: AGENT_SYSTEM_REVIEW, DEVELOPER_HANDOFF, DEVELOPER_HANDOFF_SUMMARY, DOCKER_IMPLEMENTATION, FUTURE_ARCHITECTURE_PLAN, IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN, IMPLEMENTATION_STATUS, ROADMAP_TO_100, SECURITY_AUDIT, STRATEGIC_PLAN, USENIX_SUBMISSION_NOTES + root-level architectural_review_strategic_plan.md
  • Cross-references updated: 7 user-facing docs (README.md, USER_GUIDE.md, AE_WALKTHROUGH.md, ATTACK_PACK_GUIDE.md, SCENARIO_AUTHORING.md, DEPLOYMENT.md, MAINTENANCE.md) + 5 internal docs
  • Result: docs/ = 23 user-facing docs + 3 subdirectories; docs/internal/ = 12 planning docs
  • Status: [ok] Complete

Effort: S

Pre-existing functionality — the export bundle system was already in place. Verified through integration test.

  • Status: [ok] Complete (pre-existing)

docs.text:
Effort: M

Pre-existing functionality — save_phase_checkpoint() validates JSON after write and computes SHA-256 hashes; verify_checkpoint() re-computes on restore.

  • Status: [ok] Complete (pre-existing)

Pre-existing functionality — crucible clean --all shows per-chain cleanup details.

  • Status: [ok] Complete (pre-existing)

docs.title: Item 12: Per-Attack Fidelity Table (BENCHMARKS.md)
docs.text:
Effort: XS

Added a "Per-Attack Fidelity Assessment" section to docs/BENCHMARKS.md with a table showing all 6 benchmark packs, their declared fidelity levels, upper bounds, and specific constraints.

  • Table covers: nomad_replica_fraud (L2, ceiling L3), wormhole_guardian_bypass (L2, ceiling L2), poly_network_privkey (L2, ceiling L4), horizon_multisig_drain (L2, ceiling L2), multichain_router_permit (L1, ceiling —), bnb_token_hub_proof (L2, ceiling L3)
  • Also added: Fidelity Level Legend (L0-L4 definitions)
  • Status: [ok] Complete

docs.title: Item 13: Run Tiers (scratch/saved/published)
docs.text:
Effort: M

Added run retention tiers with auto-cleanup, plus crucible publish command.

  • Files: crucible/config.py (RUN_TIERS type, CleanupConfig fields), crucible/provenance.py (run_tier in manifest), crucible/history.py (run_tier DB column), crucible/experiment.py (run_tier param), crucible/commands/experiment.py (--tier flag), crucible/commands/export.py (publish command), crucible/cleanup.py (tier-aware removal)
  • Tiers: scratch (auto-clean 7 days), saved (indefinite), published (indefinite + prominent)
  • Usage: crucible experiment run ... --tier saved | crucible publish <run_id>
  • Tests: 15 new tests in tests/unit/test_run_tiers.py
  • Status: [ok] Complete

docs.title: Item 14: JSONL Schema Validation
docs.text:
Effort: S

Bundle-time validation of all 6 JSONL artifact streams against expected schemas.

  • File: crucible/schema.py (new) — defines required/optional fields and type constraints for each stream
  • Integration: crucible verify --schemas flag, ExportBundle.create() logs warnings
  • Streams validated: run.jsonl, events.jsonl, storage_diffs.jsonl, gas_profile.jsonl, balance_diffs.jsonl, cross_chain.jsonl
  • Scans: Root-level + chain_* + bridge/ subdirectories automatically
  • Tests: 22 new tests in tests/unit/test_schema.py
  • Status: [ok] Complete

docs.title: Item 15: --from-tx Attack Scaffolder
docs.text:
Effort: L

Added --from-tx option to crucible attack init that fetches on-chain transaction metadata and populates the scaffold with real data.

  • File: crucible/commands/attack.py
  • New helper: _fetch_tx_metadata() — connects to RPC, extracts fork block, contract address, attacker address, calldata preview
  • Graceful degradation: Returns defaults (no crash) when RPC unavailable
  • Populates: evidence.yaml (fork_block, exploit_transactions, affected_contracts), plugin.py (CONTRACT_ADDRESS constant, get_code check, calldata preview), scenario.yaml (fork_blocks)
  • Usage: crucible attack init bnb_token_hub --chain bsc --from-tx 0x5ea16a... --rpc-url https://...
  • Tests: 16 new tests in tests/unit/test_attack_from_tx.py
  • Status: [ok] Complete

docs.title: Item 16: Fidelity Level Runtime Enforcement
docs.text:
Effort: M

Runtime validation that experiment behavior matches the fidelity level declared in evidence.yaml.

  • File: crucible/enforcer.py (new) — FidelityEnforcer class with level-scoped checks
  • Enforcement per level:
  • L0: None (always passes)
  • L1: Attack plugin returned a result
  • L2: Evidence chains in topology, trace artifacts present, no trace failures
  • L3: exact_tx_replay=true, exploit_transactions non-empty, tx actions in run.jsonl
  • L4: Multi-chain topology + bridge plugin + excluded_components declared
  • Integration: Post-run check in ExperimentRunner.run(), guarded by outcome == "success"
  • Flags: Fidelity enforcement enabled by default; use --no-fidelity to skip. publication.fidelity_enforcement (config) falls back to publication gates.
  • Tests: 19 new tests in tests/unit/test_enforcer.py
  • Status: [ok] Complete

docs.title: Item 17: Bridge Plugin Evidence Requirements
docs.text:
Effort: S

Extended evidence.yaml validation to bridge plugins, requiring protocol description, features list, and limitations list.

  • Files: crucible/evidence.py (BRIDGE_EVIDENCE_SECTIONS + validate_bridge_evidence()), crucible/plugin.py (bridge validation hook), crucible/commands/plugin.py (bridge warning display)
  • New file: bridges/demo_bridge/evidence.yaml — complete bridge evidence
  • Tests: 10 new tests in tests/unit/test_bridge_evidence.py
  • Status: [ok] Complete

docs.title: 5. Known Gaps & Cosmetic Issues
docs.text:
1. Docker volume artifact access — Artifacts produced by Docker experiments live inside Docker named volumes (logs_data, states_data), not directly on the host filesystem. Use docker compose run --rm orchestrator ls logs/... to inspect artifacts from Docker runs.

  2. CLI_REFERENCE.md Maintenance — The reference doc should be regenerated when commands change. Currently maintained manually.

  3. Local Foundry requirement — On systems without Foundry binaries on PATH (common on Windows), all experiments must run through Docker. The crucible doctor command correctly reports this condition.

docs.title: 6. How to Verify
docs.text:
# Run all unit tests
cd crucible-testbed
python -m pytest tests/unit/ -q

# Verify docs links
python -m pytest tests/unit/test_docs_links.py -q

# Test CLI help (verify new flags appear)
crucible verify --help                    # should show --schemas
crucible experiment run --help             # should show --tier, --name, --no-fidelity
crucible publish --help                    # should show as command
crucible attack init --help                # should show --from-tx
crucible history --help                     # should show --tier, --tag, --outcome

# Live integration test via Docker
docker compose up -d anvil-ethereum
docker compose run --rm orchestrator experiment run experiments/demo_experiment.yaml --no-baseline --tier saved --name "verify-test"
docker compose run --rm orchestrator verify logs/demo_experiment/DS-2026-xxxxx --verbose --schemas
docker compose run --rm orchestrator history --limit 3

Plus docs consolidation moves (12 files → docs/internal/) and cross-reference updates in 6 existing docs.

docs.title: Reproducibility Guide
docs.text: New to Crucible? Reproducibility is baked into the testbed's core design. The Technical Overview explains the philosophy behind determinism-by-default, cache fingerprinting, and fidelity enforcement. For the design rationale behind each reproducibility mechanism, see the Design Decisions document.

docs.title: What Makes A Crucible Run Reproducible
docs.text:
Every crucible experiment run pins the following in run_manifest.json:

Field Source Purpose git_commit git rev-parse HEAD Code version anchor git_dirty git status --porcelain Detect uncommitted changes foundry_version forge --version Anvil/forge binary version python_version sys.version Python version platform sys.platform OS identifier config_fingerprint SHA-256 of testbed.yaml Detect config drift docker_mode Detected from env Container vs native cache_mode CacheManager.mode() Cold/warm cache state cache_fingerprint SHA-256 of all cache manifests Cache identity experiment_file_hash SHA-256 of experiment YAML Experiment definition pin evidence_hash SHA-256 of evidence.yaml Attack definition pin scenario_hash SHA-256 of scenario.yaml Environment definition pin run_id DS-YYYY-NNNNN format Monotonically increasing, year-resetting counter timestamps UTC ISO-8601 Chronological anchor

docs.title: Reproducibility Modes
docs.text: Mode Description Requirements Docker (preferred) Containerized environment with pinned dependencies Docker, git Local Native Python + Foundry Python 3.11+, Foundry, archive RPC Cached fork Reuses previously fetched RPC cache Docker or local + existing .fork_cache Artifact replay Inspect exported bundle without re-execution Only the datasets/ export bundle

docs.title: Provenance Verification
docs.text:
After any experiment run, verify reproducibility by checking:

# 1. Check the run manifest
cat logs/*/<run_id>/run_manifest.json | jq '.provenance'

# 1b. Verify config fingerprint manually
# The config fingerprint is computed via get_config_fingerprint() in crucible.provenance
# It covers testbed.yaml and active topology settings.

# 2. Check log integrity
cat logs/*/<run_id>/log_integrity.json

# 3. Run twice and diff
crucible diff <run_a> <run_b>

# 4. Export and verify bundle
crucible export <run_id>
crucible verify datasets/<run_id>

docs.title: Cache Policy & Determinism
docs.text:
Cache state affects timing but NOT results. Strict policy ensures cache fingerprints match the current config:

# testbed.yaml
fork_cache:
  enabled: true
  cache_policy: strict     # invalidates on provider/block/version mismatch
  # or
  cache_policy: permissive # warns but doesn't invalidate

The cache_mode in run_manifest.json records whether the run used cold cache (first fork), warm cache (subsequent forks), or budget cache (RPC budget-limited mode).

docs.title: Publishing A Run
docs.text:
# 1. Run with publication gates
crucible experiment run experiments/attack.yaml --publication-mode

# 2. Export the bundle
crucible export <run_id> --format tar-gz

# 3. Verify the export
crucible verify datasets/<run_id>
cat datasets/<run_id>/bundle_hash.txt

The export bundle is self-contained — copy it anywhere and verify integrity without the original workspace.

docs.title: Export Bundle Structure
docs.text: The exported bundle (.tar.gz or uncompressed directory) built by crucible.export.ExportBundle contains: - artifact_bundle_manifest.json: Hashes of every file in the bundle. - run_manifest.json: Full provenance and execution metadata. - evidence.yaml: The attacker's claims and fidelity level. - plugin.yaml: Attack plugin configuration. - scenario.yaml: Environment preconditions. - log_integrity.json: Original runtime hashes for sub-artifacts. - All JSONL streams (events.jsonl, storage_diffs.jsonl, gas_profile.jsonl, run.jsonl, etc.) - (Optional) .protected marker indicating publication status.

docs.title: Lessons Learned From Validation (June 2026)
docs.text: Across two validation rounds — initial A-Z validation and a full attack-pack testing session — 21 bugs were found and fixed across 5 categories. Every issue here caused a real experiment failure.

docs.title: Infrastructure & Docker Issues (6 bugs)
docs.text: # Issue Where Symptom Fix 1 Foundry not installed in Docker image Dockerfile anvil not found on PATH Added foundryup --install nightly to Dockerfile 2 Wrong foundryup flag Dockerfile Build failed with foundryup --nightly Changed to foundryup --install nightly 3 ANVIL_PORT missing from daemon services docker-compose.yml Docker healthcheck failing Added ANVIL_PORT to each Anvil service 4 Entrypoint is crucible, not shell docker-compose.yml sh -c fails Use --entrypoint bash 5 Stale meta files in Docker volume states/ volume anvil_dumpState on wrong port Clean states/*.meta.json before each fork 6 Daemon Anvil fork block mismatch docker-compose.yml Experiment uses wrong fork block Use single-container mode instead

docs.title: Code Bugs (3 bugs)
docs.text: # Issue Where Symptom Fix 7 Missing import logging / logger instrumentation.py NameError: name 'logger' is not defined Added import and logger definition 8 Wrong python_script path resolution scenario_steps.py Script not found (only checked scenario dir) Check workspace root first, then scenario dir 9 LogManager.step() message kwarg conflict poly_network_privkey.py step() got multiple values for 'message' Remove message= kwarg, inline into positional arg

docs.title: Research Data Errors — Contract Addresses & Fork Blocks (4 bugs)
docs.text: # Issue Where Symptom Fix 10 WormholeCore address was an EOA wormhole_guardian_bypass.py WormholeCore has no code Corrected to verified contract address on Etherscan 11 EthCrossChainManager was an EOA poly_network_privkey.py Manager has no code Corrected to verified address 0x838bf9E9... 12 EthCrossChainData address wrong poly_network_privkey.py Data has no code Corrected to verified address 0xcF2afe10... 13 Poly Network fork block + exploit tx wrong evidence.yaml Off by 631 blocks, wrong tx hash Fork block 12,996,693, exploit tx 0x5a8b2152...

docs.title: Round 2: Full Attack-Pack Testing (7 bugs + 1 enhancement)
Framework Bugs (4 bugs)
docs.text: # Issue Where Symptom Fix 14 Auto-merge kills chain but never restarts it experiment.py Connection refused after fork block update Auto-merge now re-forks chains after terminating old processes 15 Port TIME_WAIT after killing old Anvil experiment.py New Anvil can't bind — port in TIME_WAIT Poll port every 500ms for up to 10 seconds before restarting 16 Checkpoints snapshot dead chains checkpoint.py anvil_dumpState on stale meta file Filter checkpoint to only snapshot chains in active topology 17 Fidelity failures hidden from console experiment.py Only [fail] shown, no reasons Print specific failure reasons inline below [fail] line

docs.title: Bridge Deployment Bugs (3 bugs)
docs.text: # Issue Where Symptom Fix 18 Double 0x prefix in bytecode 3 bridge plugins RPC error: invalid value "0x0x6080..." Remove "0x" + prefix — Foundry bytecode already has it 19 Precompile deployer address fails on archive nodes 3 bridge plugins anvil_setBalance fails on 0x...0001 Wrap per-chain deploy in try/except; skip chain gracefully 20 Placeholder string in bridge evidence.yaml nomad_bridge_demo/evidence.yaml get_code("deployed by bridge plugin") fails Use empty list [] for dynamically deployed contracts

docs.title: CLI Enhancement (1 feature)
docs.text: # Issue Where Symptom Fix 21 No way to bypass auto-merge commands/experiment.py Auto-merge always runs even when undesirable Added --no-auto-merge CLI flag

docs.title: Root Cause Analysis
docs.text:
Round 1 (Most common): Contract addresses that were actually EOAs (user wallets), not contracts — 3 out of 13 bugs.

Round 2 (Most common): Framework lifecycle bugs — 4 out of 7 bugs were in experiment.py and checkpoint.py, where auto-merge, port management, and checkpointing didn't handle edge cases. The remaining 3 were bridge plugin issues, plus 1 CLI enhancement (--no-auto-merge flag).

Attack Wrong Address (What It Actually Was) Correct Address (Verified Contract) WormholeCore 0x98f3c9e6...360aA59... (an EOA) 0x98f3c9e6...36bAAd0... (Etherscan [ok] verified) EthCrossChainManager 0xa2526e9d...a59275 (an EOA) 0x838bf9E9...F928270 (Etherscan [ok] verified) EthCrossChainData 0x4f6c4662... (wrong address) 0xcF2afe10... (Etherscan [ok] verified as EthCrossChainData)

Before adding any contract address to an attack pack: - [ ] Paste the address into Etherscan - [ ] Verify the page shows "Contract" (not "Address (EOA)") - [ ] Check the contract has non-zero transaction history - [ ] Verify the contract name matches what you expect

Before setting a fork block: - [ ] Find the exploit transaction on the block explorer - [ ] Note the block number - [ ] Set fork block = exploit block - 1 - [ ] Verify the vulnerable contracts exist at the fork block

Before running an experiment: - [ ] Clean stale meta files: rm -f states/*.meta.json (if using Docker) - [ ] Set the correct fork block in testbed.yaml or testbed.local.yaml - [ ] Verify the RPC provider supports archive depth at your fork block - [ ] Use --entrypoint bash when chaining Docker commands - [ ] Run twice and diff: crucible diff <run_a> <run_b> - [ ] Check all 4 metrics (gas, storage, events, balances) are equal

docs.title: Baseline System
docs.text: Crucible uses a dual-verification baseline system that ensures reproducibility across runs:

docs.title: How Baselines Work
docs.text:
1. Capture — After forking and bootstrapping, crucible baseline capture <scenario>:
  2. Dumps the full EVM state for each chain via anvil_dumpState
  3. Canonicalizes and SHA-256 hashes the state
  4. Captures a live state hash from the running chain

  5. Writes a baseline manifest YAML with both hashes

  6. Verify — Before running an experiment, crucible baseline verify <scenario>:

  7. Re-computes the snapshot file hash and compares it to the manifest
  8. If the chain is running: also compares the live chain state against the captured state hash

  9. This dual check catches both file corruption AND environment drift

  10. Restore — crucible reset --mode soft loads the baseline snapshot back into the running chain via anvil_loadState (<5s)

docs.title: Baseline Storage
docs.text:
states/baseline/
├── <scenario>.baseline.yaml      # Manifest with hashes and metadata
├── <scenario>.<chain>.json       # Full EVM state snapshot
└── <scenario>.<chain2>.json      # One per chain in topology

docs.title: When Baselines Go Stale
docs.text:
Situation Detection Fix Changed RPC provider Live state hash mismatch baseline verify catches it Changed fork block State completely different baseline verify catches it Changed topology (different chains) Missing chain snapshots Re-capture with baseline capture Snapshot file deleted/corrupted File hash mismatch Re-capture with baseline capture 
# Capture a new baseline after environment changes
crucible setup --topology single
crucible baseline capture my_scenario --topology single

docs.title: Cache System & Fingerprinting
docs.text: Crucible caches fork state from archive RPCs to speed up repeated runs. The cache system has its own provenance tracking:

docs.title: Cache Policy
docs.text:
Configured in testbed.yaml:

fork_cache:
  enabled: true
  path: ".fork_cache"
  cache_policy: permissive  # or "strict"
  • strict — Invalidates cache on ANY mismatch (provider, foundry version, config fingerprint). Ensures bit-identical forks but may re-fetch frequently.
  • permissive — Warns on mismatch but doesn't invalidate. Faster for development, uses cache even if config changed slightly.

docs.title: Cache Provenance
docs.text:
Each cache entry has a manifest tracking: - RPC provider URL (fingerprinted, not stored raw) - Fork block number - Chain ID - Foundry version at time of caching - Config fingerprint (SHA-256 of testbed.yaml)

# Inspect cache entries
crucible cache inspect

# Compute aggregate fingerprint (SHA-256 of all manifests)
crucible cache fingerprint

# Verify a specific entry
crucible cache verify --chain ethereum --fork-block 15259100

# Clear stale cache
crucible cache clean --chain ethereum

The cache_mode in run_manifest.json records whether the run used cold (first fork), warm (subsequent), or budget (RPC-limited) cache.

docs.title: Publication Gates
docs.text: Publication gates enforce research-quality standards before a run completes. They are optional but recommended for formal publications:

docs.title: Enabling Publication Gates
docs.text:
crucible experiment run experiments/attack.yaml --publication-mode

Or configure them to always run:

# testbed.yaml
publication:
  enabled: true
  require_foundry_pinned: true        # Foundry version must match testbed.yaml
  require_baseline_verified: true     # Baseline must pass verify() before run
  require_provenance_complete: true   # Git commit, config fingerprint must be available
  fidelity_enforcement: false         # When True, enforces fidelity at runtime (default check-based)

docs.title: What Each Gate Checks
docs.text:
Gate Checks Fails When Foundry pinned Installed Foundry version matches testbed.yaml Version mismatch, or required_version not set Baseline verified Latest baseline passes verify() Missing baseline, hash mismatch, live state drift Provenance complete Git commit available, config fingerprint computed Dirty working tree, missing .git, config parse failure Fidelity enforcement Runtime behavior matches declared fidelity level Missing trace artifacts, wrong chain topology, no exploit transactions

Note: Each gate evaluation returns a GateResult object containing passed (bool), reason (str), and fatal (bool).

docs.title: Run Tiers
docs.text:
Every experiment run is assigned a retention tier that controls cleanup behavior:

Tier Default For Auto-Cleaned Preserved By scratch All runs (default) After 7 days Nothing saved --tier saved flag Never Explicit flag published crucible publish Never .protected marker in dataset 
# Run and immediately save
crucible experiment run experiments/attack.yaml --tier saved

# Promote an existing run to published
crucible publish DS-2026-00155

# The run is now protected from all auto-cleanup

docs.title: Publication Lifecycle
docs.text:
1. Run experiment          → crucible experiment run ... --tier saved
2. Verify artifacts        → crucible verify logs/<experiment>/<run_id>
3. Promote to published    → crucible publish <run_id>
4. Export publication bundle → crucible export <run_id> --format tar-gz
5. Upload to Zenodo/archive → Upload datasets/<run_id>.tar.gz

docs.title: Pre-Reproduction Workflow
docs.text:
Before running any reproduction, set up your environment:

# 1. Initialize workspace
crucible init

# 2. Validate environment
crucible doctor

# 3. Set up and capture baseline
crucible setup --topology <topology>
crucible baseline capture <scenario> --topology <topology>

# 4. Verify baseline
crucible baseline verify <scenario>
